title: Same-Sign Tetralepton Signature at $־¼$TRISTAN
author_string: Lin-Kun Yan, Zhi-Long Han, Feng-Lan Shao, Fa-Xin Yang
abstract: Naturally tiny neutrino masses can be explained by the low scale seesaw with heavy neutral lepton $N$ coupling to the neutrinophilic Higgs doublet $־¦_־½$, which obtains a much smaller vacuum expectation value than the standard Higgs doublet $־¦$. Within this model, the neutrino masses originate from the new Yukawa interaction $y \overline{L}\tilde־¦_־½N$. In this paper, we propose the novel same-sign tetralepton signature at the 2 TeV same-sign muon mode $־¼^+־¼^+$ of $־¼$TRISTAN. We investigate two distinct channels of this signature, which are both generated by the Yukawa interaction $y \overline{L}\tilde־¦_־½N$. One is from the pair production of charged Higgs $־¼^+־¼^+\to H^+ H^+\to ־¼^+N +־¼^+ N\to ־¼^+ ־¼^+ jj + ־¼^+ ־¼^+ jj\to 4־¼^+ + 4j$, and the other one is from the single production of charged Higgs $־¼^+־¼^+ \to ־¼^+ N H^+ \to ־¼^+N +־¼^+ N\to ־¼^+ ־¼^+ jj + ־¼^+ ־¼^+ jj\to 4־¼^+ + 4j$. We then perform a detailed simulation of this same-sign tetralepton signature, and obtain the promising region at $־¼$TRISTAN.
